The graphics, lighting and weather effects are some of the best we've seen in a racing game, but of course it's all about the cars. Although this is an arcade racer that does not focus on realism but rather on short and fast action, the cars behave truthfully and react differently whether you are driving on a track, on the street or off road. The controls have been kept simple, making the game acc

Forza Horizon 5 still is a great racing game, but there’s no denying that the series is in a torpor-like state. It’s obvious that Playground is afraid not only to innovate, but to make any sort of significant change to a successful formula. [Issue#257, p.64]

I don't think there's a motoring enthusiast who won't be excited about Forza Horizon 5, with a slight caveat for those who have had their fill of Forza Horizon 4 and might want more of the same - though honestly, I can't imagine how the formula could be improved upon, which in the arcade driving department is bordering on perfection. It promises and delivers an incomparably customizable driving ex
A masterful open-world racer in an unforgettable setting - would have benefited from a little more courage to innovate.

It’s no surprise that Forza Horizon puts itself right into the mix with 2021’s Game of the Year contenders. Playground Games has been getting it right for a decade now. This is the definitive experience in the series so far, one that excels both on PC and the Xbox Series X. Minimal issues, such as server connectivity, fail to spoil the party that is just crammed with happiness and adventure. A num

Familiar, excellent, and polished to a degree that hardly seems possible. If you love taking vintage Porsches for joyrides through environments that’d have a poet bawling then you're going to be very happy here.

Forza Horizon 5 is the racing game for everyone, which takes outsiders from the racing genre and turns them into followers of the speed, and for those who are already fans, it offers an almost endless variety of activities and challenges. Also very remarkable is the representation of Mexico, which offers vibrant landscapes full of incredible details that only those who live in Mexico will recogniz

Forza Horizon 5 is still an incredible racing game, one only overshadowed by the brilliance of its predecessor. Those with a lot of time behind the wheel in Forza Horizon 4 may feel this is one step forward for two steps back, but there's still a ton of fun to be had in this high-octane adventure.

With Forza Horizon 5, the racing series makes its first stop in Mexico. It delivers breathtaking vistas, fast action, and the hook: the new EventLab mode that lets the community create cool, custom challenges.

The detour into Mexico is a sight for sore eyes after a three-year stay in the rather dreary United Kingdom of Horizon 4. Innovation is about the last thing you’ll find on the Forza Horizon 5 buffet, but there is no denying that this is the best software available for Microsoft’s Series X|S consoles and, for people who have strong PCs, the best reason yet to pony up for a 4K 120Hz HDR display.

Forza Horizon 5 has carried the tradition of consuming my time inside a genre that normally doesn't interest me. Playground Games has displayed technical wizardry with this game and while it doesn’t change the formula to any significant extent, the experience found within Forza Horizon 5 is killer.

¡Ay, caramba! Forza Horizon 5 is maturing under the Mexican sun a recipe concocted by Playground Games that has already delighted millions of pilots. Like an antojito, Forza Horizon 5 can be enjoyed in a good mood with anyone, anytime. Rolling over everything that has already been technically done and taking advantage of the Xbox Series X springboard to stamp the skies, Microsoft's software climbs

While Forza Horizon 5 launches with a score of "only" 9.0, it has potential for even more. All Playground Games have to do is to eliminate bugs and bring back some missing cars, which could be done in a few months, with several updates. What about a perfect 10/10? It would require a multitude of further improvements to AI and physics. But perhaps Forza Horizon 6 will achieve that. I surely hope so

Playground's singular series gets a lush new setting, but otherwise remains committed to tuning an already winning formula. But even a marginal improvement on the best racing series around is worth celebrating.

Forza Horizon 5 is yet another step up for the series and arguably the best racing game ever made. Offering a host of content across a fantastic representation of Mexico, its varied terrain and scenery, there's so much to explore and race. A massive roster of cars will perform all of these, each feeling different from the others, with a variety of upgrade and tuning options almost matching those o

Forza Horizon 5 is a masterclass example of how to do an open-world racing game, bar none. Thanks to its deeply rich map, wealth of content, and fantastic visuals, it's the best racing experience I've had yet.

You can whine about the lack of revolution, but when everything works perfectly fine, maybe there is no reason to change it. Forza Horizon 5 is definitely the best racing game out there and has no competition. This is, by far, the biggest car adventure you can have on your screen.
Forza Horizon 5 is undoubtedly a visual showcase and technical benchmark for PC and Xbox Series X owners. If you have the hardware to run this game on PC, this is the choice to make.

Forza Horizon 5 is a formidable game, with a spectacular graphics and an overwhelming amount of content. But above all, it is a fun title that dignifies the fun in video games with no greater pretension than that, to have the best time possible.

Forza Horizon 5 is built to deliver hour after hour of glossy, beautiful fun. Hit the race mechanics as deeply as you like. Zip around the amazingly beautiful map in a Corvette for no reason at all. Find secrets. Build out your car collection. Get rich. Have a good time the way you want. Go anywhere, do anything, just don’t skip this stellar game.
pipeline_summary:
포르자 호라이즌 5는 역대 최고의 운전 경험을 제공하며, 도전적인 드라이브를 원하는 플레이어에게 적합합니다.
장점: 게임의 조작감은 좋고, 패드 새로 사서 하면 몰입감이 너무 좋다는 평가가 있습니다. / 장르 입문작으로 접근하기 좋다는 반응이 있습니다. / 낮은 사양에서도 실행 가능해 접근성이 좋다는 반응이 있습니다.
단점: 퀘스트 및 안내가 복잡하다는 단점이 있습니다. / 마이크로계정 찾기 문제가 발생한다는 평가가 있습니다. / 윈도우 계정 로그인 버그가 있다는 보고가 있습니다. / 가격이 비싸다는 의견이 있습니다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 오픈 월드 레이싱 게임의 정수, 아름다운 멕시코 풍경과 다양한 차량 수집의 재미

장점: 
- 아름다운 멕시코 풍경을 배경으로 한 오픈 월드 탐험
- 다양한 차량 수집 및 커스터마이징의 재미
- 풍부한 콘텐츠와 엔드게임 활동

단점: 
- 일부 플레이어에게 지루할 수 있는 진행 방식
- 서버 연결 문제 및 버그 발생 사례
- 그래픽 사양이 다소 높게 요구됨

So much damage has already been done to the game simply by calling it Overwatch 2. From the second of its announcement, Blizzard were at the mercy of players' expectations for a full-fledged sequel. And when they compare those two side-by-side screenshots and see virtually no difference, that's a major disappointment and perhaps a turn-off for many players. For a content update, Overwatch 2 does a

Enjoying Overwatch 2 is an exercise in cautious optimism — not just in the future direction of its ever-changing lore and world, but in the idea that years of new content will ultimately deliver on the promise of a full sequel.
Overwatch 2 shines and pops with updated graphics and gorgeous new maps, but its deviation from the source material is impossible to ignore.

Overwatch 2 is a great game, but it is held back by its own identity crisis, lack of content, and questionable choices. Despite its blemishes, Overwatch 2 is a must-try experience, especially if you are playing with a dedicated squad of friends. Whether you click with it right away, grow to love it, or try it and bounce off, the core gameplay of Overwatch 2 is undeniably fun regardless of its exte
An aimless-feeling revamp of 2016's best multiplayer game, slightly coarsened by free-to-play grinding.

Overwatch 2 has a branding problem. The “2” implies something new or different or at least fundamentally distinguishable from what came before. But as it is, it’s just not, particularly for a numbered sequel to one of the most popular shooters of all time. Playing rekindled all those old, happy feelings I hadn’t experienced since the early days of Overwatch, and part of that is because it just fee

Don't be fooled by Overwatch 2's title: it's nowhere near a sequel, but a relaunch instead. Blizzard clearly took this opportunity to update (not necessarily enhance) pretty much every aspect of the game and catch up with every other hot, current multiplayer game. Is that a bad thing? Hell naw. OW2 is wildly fun.
Overwatch 2 revitalizes the stagnant hero shooter, but misunderstands what made it great.

Overwatch 2 is the awaited game that keeps on the legacy of the 2016 hero-shooter of Blizzard. With little news and a lot of problems with servers and bugs, the experience of the game has been a complete nightmare for a lot of users. But, it's not a secret that the game core is mainly the same as before: tons of fun playing with friends and alone, with an even bigger roaster of heroes and maps, an

Overwatch 2 replaces its predecessor with a similar, yet different experience, but its future feels uncertain as the franchise moves toward a more aggressive monetization structure with little to distinguish itself from every other live-service game.

Overwatch 2 does a lot of good, but at the same time almost everything went wrong before, around and after launch. It's hard to see the wood for all those trees of mistakes, when somewhere in that mess is still hiding one of the best shooters of the moment. Part two makes some much-needed improvements that make it play better in theory than ever before. Unfortunately, in practice, the many bugs an

Overwatch 2’s launch issues are still awful. Like most of you, dear readers, I didn’t get an advance copy of the game or any special connection privileges. I was with all of you in the trenches, dealing with the constant error messages and inexhaustible queues. The game’s new battle pass system remains questionable and concerning.But I am very pleased with the core gameplay changes and where the g
Overwatch 2 is as fun and fast-paced as the original game, but its free-to-play structure sullies the experience a bit.

As things stand, Overwatch 2 is not so much a sequel as it is an update to the first chapter. Net of the changes made, we cannot consider what we have seen so far as something fully satisfactory or capable of justifying the number "to the right" of the title. The changes introduced are appreciable and the business model adopted could give new life to the franchise, but unfortunately, between the a

Although it is still a very fun and enjoyable online FPS game to play, the fact that the in-game economy is so greedy and focused on exploiting the player, and there isn't anything in game that deserves that "2" in the name lowered the overall score for me. Unfortunately Battle Passes are now a part of these games, but Overwatch 2 has exploited that a lot. At least base game is free.
Given a new lease of life by those whip-smart changes, in the moment-to-moment Overwatch 2 sings. [Issue#378, p.108]

Overwatch 2 is a free to play game, and I’d definitely recommend trying it out if you haven’t to see how you feel about the gameplay and to see how well it runs on Steam Deck. Despite its flaws, it is still worth playing, but some of the decisions with how certain content has been handled in-game are weird. Overwatch 2 is yet to reach the highs of the original Overwatch, but it is still a great mu

While Overwatch 2 is still a good time, and an improvement over the original, it feels like less of a sequel and more of an update. There's some solid changes to the foundations of Overwatch, but a noticeable lack of new content, archaic game modes, and balancing issues leave something to be desired.
There is a good game hiding underneath Overwatch 2's predatory shell, one that is soon to evolve into an even more massive headache for players as the game evolves.
While Overwatch 2 adds a host of heroes and features to Blizzard’s iconic FPS game, its PvP feels more like a simple content update than a full blown sequel.

For long-term players, Overwatch 2 may feel more like a glorified update with problematic progression and roadblocks to enjoyment that didn't exist in the original game. In its current state, Overwatch 2 is more product than art, but there is still plenty of time to turn things around between now and whenever the game's 1.0 version launches.

pipeline_summary:
오버워치 2는 탐험 콘텐츠와 재미있는 게임플레이를 제공하지만, 밸런스 문제와 최적화 문제, 버그 등에 대한 플레이어들의 불만이 있습니다.
장점: 오버워치 2는 탐험 콘텐츠와 재미있는 게임플레이를 제공합니다. / 게임의 밸런스가 잘 잡혀 있습니다. / 콘솔로 플레이하면 학살당하는 일이 적다는 의견이 있습니다.
단점: 게임의 밸런스 문제와 일부 영웅들의 약한 성능에 대해 불만이 있습니다. / 10주년 이벤트의 반복적인 콘텐츠에 대해 불만이 있습니다. / 한 리뷰는 게임의 진행 장벽과 어려움에 대해 언급했습니다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 오버워치는 중독성 있는 게임플레이를 가진 인기 있는 팀 기반 슈팅 게임이지만, 밸런스 문제, 매칭 시스템의 어려움, 및 공격적인 수익 창출 전략으로 비판을 받고 있습니다.

장점: 
- 빠른 게임플레이와 다양한 영웅들이 등장하는 팀 기반 슈팅 게임
- 업데이트와 새로운 콘텐츠 추가가 지속적으로 이루어짐
- 커뮤니티가 활성화되어 있고, 많은 플레이어들이 게임을 즐김

단점: 
- 밸런스 문제가 지속적으로 제기되고 있음
- 매칭 시스템이 어려워서 고수들과 신규 플레이어 간의 격차가 큼
- 공격적인 수익 창출 전략으로 인해 플레이어들이 불만을 가짐

Monster Hunter Rise is a great game that’s even better on PC, but it’s hard to recommend if you’ve already played it on the Switch and don’t have unlimited free time. If you managed to get a Steam Deck preorder, though, this could well be the perfect game to christen it with for anyone looking to get into the series. It’s just unfortunate that Monster Hunter enthusiasts who already jumped on Rise

Overall, Monster Hunter Rise on PC is another perfect example of why the series belongs on computer. It offers all the excellent experiences found on the Nintendo Switch, albeit with improved visuals, better resolution, a myriad of performance options, and greater controller support. Hopefully there comes a day when PC players get to experience a Monster Hunter title at the same time as console pl

For the most part, though, Monster Hunter Rise is another stonking addition to the series, and in my books a more than worthy successor to Monster Hunter: World. There's a generous and playful sense of freedom here that keeps combat and exploration feeling fresh, and the momentum of its hunting-led missions means you're rarely spinning your wheels as you seek out that last elusive armour part. It'

Monster Hunter Rise on PC is just a different way to play a fantastic game. And while that’s great news for PC players who’ve never bought into Nintendo’s ecosystem, its graphics fail to impress, even on the most impressive of hardware.

Monster Hunter Rise is a brilliant addition to the Monster Hunter lineup. It delivers incredible combat with newly added monsters and highly-desired mobility. However, it does suffer from messy systems, a mediocre story and can become quite grindy. Despite these flaws though, I have had countless hours of fun and will continue to do so. The co-op aspect just adds even more enjoyment to an already

My favorite MH game, its so peak every single step of the way, I love all the new monsters added not just here but in Sunbreak as well (MALZENO THE GOAT) and the best part is that it remains well optimized while also looking pretty good tho im not a gal who cares about graphics much so idk :P

Not better than the past games sadly but at least it’s way better than World and i can only presume Wilds as well. There’s no shot in hell we’re getting those games outside the 3ds so we gottajust take what we can get i suppose.

♥ game. Mhw copy pasted, with a few improvements but with the same old metric ♥ of issues. Multiplayer is a nightmare. Menu navigation is dumb as ♥. Hitboxes are stupid as hell. Cant load game soon as it turns on keeps trying to force full screen and stays black. I can hear the audio and see it in-between flashes. I reinstalled graphics drivers and game made no difference. Tried googling it found no answer. No other gamer in my library has any issues like this from very graphics demanding to barely any. Bad game
Monster Hunter Rise’s combat is excellent, and the PC version is virtually a perfect port of the Switch version. As someone who had never played a Monster Hunter game before, I’m hooked.

Monster Hunter Rise on PC is the definitive version of an excellent entry in this long running franchise and whether you are a veteran of the series or a rookie, looking to jump in for the first time, it’s the perfect opportunity to grab a weapon and start hunting some monsters.

The PC port for Monster Hunter Rise is the definitive way to experience this game. I am excited to see where the franchise goes from here, although I will find it hard to slide back into any Monster Hunter game without the new traversal and streamlined elements. Whether you want to hunt alone or play with your friends, play “Fashion Hunter” or experience the main quests, there is something for eve

Monster Hunter is a series about systems—Capcom can’t escape that, no matter how much marketing and PR they throw at it. But it isn’t a series about getting it either, as much as its staunchest adherents would have you believe. It’s about feeling it. And Monster Hunter Rise feels good—simple as that.

When considering the PC version of Rise, it’s hard not to compare it directly to the as-yet unmatched juggernaut that is Monster Hunter World – a competition that I don’t think Rise wins. That said, Monster Hunter Rise is still an enjoyable, well-crafted game with some compelling innovations that make it a wonderful addition to the Monster Hunter franchise. Plus, you can make a fire-breathing T-Re

Monster Hunter Rise remains a stellar title on the PC, hardly losing any ground from the greatness it already achieved earlier this year on the Nintendo Switch. The game looks better than ever now that powerful hardware improves the graphics, giving everything from the monsters to the environments a nice polish. Unfortunately, however, the new format doesn’t always take the various kinds of player
Monster Hunter Rise stands toe-to-toe with World, especially on PC. Its end-game grind may not be as robust, but a serious focus on fun and community over narrative and fidelity pays off.

The PC version of Monster Hunter Rise is a solid port that in general does not differ much from what we saw on Switch. Therefore, praise and complaints about the Switch edition mostly still apply. It’s a bit disappointing that the year that has passed between the releases brought us little more than graphical improvements, but the game itself is still stellar.

With an accelerated combat system, some light grind and well-designed monsters, Monster Hunter Rise will provide you with many hours of adrenaline-filled fun. The experience that this game provides in multiplayer is unique, regardless of the complicated formation of the party. Definitely a version that every PC gamer should have in their Steam library.
Monster Hunter Rise blends the best elements from earlier series entries, and delivers a streamlined, action-packed, monster-slaying experience.

I strongly recommend Monster Hunter Rise, but this isn’t World. It’s harder, less environmentally appealing, and more focused on the rivalry between the hunters and the monsters. Gaining the pieces to build that armor or make that weapon is sometimes a bit more of a grind as the RNG isn’t quite as good but the satisfaction as always is there and strong. But amazing combat and great rewards keep th

Monster Hunter Rise combines all of the quality of life features introduced in World while keeping the best parts of the earlier games in the series, resulting in the most refined Monster Hunter title to date.

Monster Hunter Rise has lost none of its appeal in moving from a Nintendo Switch screen to a PC one. In Ultrawide, with an unlocked frame-rate, it’s the best way to play what may be the franchise’s best entry yet. If you’re looking for deep RPG systems, a T-Rex that breathes fire, and a huge hammer to smack it in the head with, then Monster Hunter Rise is essential.
Indeed, although it is identical in terms of content to the Nintendo Switch version on PC, it is a total success and remains an excellent gateway for beginners.

Monster Hunter Rise is probably the best game in the series. It isn’t quite as accessible as World, but long term it offers much more varied combat and a richer experience. Its world and gameplay are insanely engaging and fun. Technically, the port to PC results in solid, smooth gameplay and marginally better visuals. If you have a choice, it’s definitely the way to play, the best and most complet
Monster Hunter Rise isn’t as ambitious as its predecessor, but it still manages to build on the franchise in meaningful ways.

Monster Hunter Rise is a big step forward for the Monster Hunter series, both in terms of its design and the quality of its PC port. The port is nothing groundbreaking, but the MH team has done themselves proud with this, and I’m just glad to be able to play this game at a high framerate.

Beyond mounting monsters and using the wirebug to wall-run in spectacular fashion, not much else distinguishes Monster Hunter Rise from the installments that came before. It has enough endgame content to keep you occupied long after the credits roll (if you don’t mind copious amounts of grinding) and multiplayer is still the optimal way to play, but the excitement of my early hunts waned before lo
Improves on what was already a fantastic game in almost every way.

Monster Hunter Rise was an excellent chapter of the series on Switch, but the PC version plays in a completely different category. If you liked Monster Hunter World, you'll love Rise. Yes, even if the thought of starting all over again terrifies you.

It seems that it was a simple task to bring Monster Hunter Rise to PC because the result was pretty okay, especially when it comes to performance. The only bad thing is that its graphics don't look better, even if the resolution is 4K. Nevertheless, it's worth your time, you're going to have fun from first to the last hunt.
MH Rise refines the combat system with clever innovations that both veterans and newcomers will enjoy - if they can overlook the low-grade graphics and story.

It is an HD upgrade, but its graphic improvements are enough to amply enhance the monster hunting experience of Monster Hunter: Rise, and this makes it one of the best action games on PC. If you have not played this game on Nintendo Switch, I strongly recommend you buy this game and experience the new action system and Wirebugs in Monster Hunter: Rise. But if you are a seasoned hunter who has defe

Monster Hunter Rise is a great game with an even better PC port that I believe is the best way to enjoy the game especially if you're a hardcore fan of the series that prefers to hunt monsters in the most optimal way possible.

Monster Hunter Rise arrives on PC in dazzling form, complete with support for 4K resolution, unlocked frame-rate and ultrawide monitors. Too bad we’re not able to carry the old save into this new version that will force you to start all over again.
An excellent port that presents a solid, complete experience. Super fast load times, improved performance and a ton of content make Monster Hunter Rise on PC the definitive version.
Monster Hunter Rise is at its best on the PC. The extra power brings a smoother, prettier experience to an already cracking game.
Monster Hunter Rise looks better on PC, runs better on PC, has more control & online options on PC, and arrives with more content at its respective launch.

Monster Hunter Rise on PC has all the updates and new content added to the Switch version right from the get go with vastly improved performance and graphics. While the visuals could've been improved further, this is still the best way to play Monster Hunter Rise.
Monster Hunter Rise on PC is the best place to play this incredible creature battler. If the series was looking for a permanent home, PC players would be more than happy to see it moving in.

With the streamlining of some of the most annoying aspects of the Monster Hunter experience and some excellent additions to the formula like Wirebugs and Switch Skills, Monster Hunter Rise reaches the heights of the best entries in the series with ease. While not all of the changes and new features have been for the best, like Rampage Quests, their general quality level, and the amazing PC port, d

Overall, the PC port of Monster Hunter Rise is superior to the Nintendo Switch version, thanks to the PC enhancements. It has some of the best combat mechanics in the Monster Hunter series, the new monsters are an absolute delight to hunt, and the improved frame rate and resolutions enrich the hunting experience. It does have a couple of flaws, but they are minor issues in the grand scheme of thin
An impressive port, with a good amount of graphical options and great optimization. Indeed the best way to enjoy Rise's amazing combat system.

Almost a year after the debut of Monster Hunter Rise on Switch, the expectation of fans on PC has been rewarded with a valuable port, which not only preserves the merits of the original version but enhances them with a good collection of technical improvements. Net of some fluctuations in the overall yield of the proposal, the new edition of Rise therefore has all the credentials to offer the peop
This is a fantastic adaptation of a fantastic game, that plays and looks better than ever on PC.

Monster Hunter Rise is another entertaining entry in the venerated franchise that continues to scratch a unique itch. New players will be eased into the franchise and returning players will enjoy hunting with friends as well. Capcom has also delivered yet another good port that definitely makes this version the one to play.

A hundred hours in, Rise does feel a little rote. The lack of quest variety and endgame options besides grinding for talismans has begun to wear on me somewhat. But the journey of getting to this point has been nothing short of an incredible experience. From zipping through the air to bludgeon monsters in the face to setting out on gathering runs with my trusty palamute, the minute-to-minute gamep

Thanks to a beautifully realised setting and some genuinely innovative combat additions, Monster Hunter Rise is fun from start to finish. If you haven’t played a Monster Hunter game yet, this is the best one to start with.

Monster Hunter Rise is a successful and worthy port, its fantastic gameplay and content more than compensate for the somewhat outdated graphics on PC. It's still clean and rather pleasant to look at during the fights, and especially very fluid. Those who enjoyed Monster Hunter World shouldn't hesitate to jump in, it's an equally valid game in the license, even if it's a bit different. For the othe

This PC port fulfills its role very well because it cancels a lot of limitations of the Switch version. The framerate is no longer stuck at 30 frames per second, the resolution can go up to 4K, some textures are much more detailed, and loading times are noticeably reduced. However, we should not expect either that this former Nintendo exclusive will miraculously transform into graphic mastery wort
Monster Hunter: Rise is arguably the greatest entry in Capcom's flagship series, and a game that simply never stops giving.
pipeline_summary:
오래 기억할 만큼 강한 만족감을 줬다는 반응이 있습니다.
장점: 빠른 포텐과 속도감을 즐기는 플레이어에게 적합합니다. / 성장하며 변화하는 플레이 스타일을 경험할 수 있습니다. / 다양한 무기를 사용할 수 있습니다. / 쾌적한 게임플레이를 제공합니다.
단점: 일본풍 배경이 과하게 느껴질 때가 있습니다. / 무게감 있는 타격감이 사라져 게임이 너무 가벼워집니다. / 엔드 콘텐츠가 지루하고 짜치는 편입니다. / 조작감이 이상한 부분이 있습니다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 몬스터 헌터 시리즈의 팬이라면 놓칠 수 없는 게임, 몬스터 헌터 라이즈는 새로운 조작감과 다양한 몬스터로 즐거운 헌팅 경험을 제공한다.

장점: 
- 새로운 조작감과 속도감 있는 전투 / 다양한 몬스터와 풍부한 콘텐츠 / 개선된 그래픽과 성능

단점: 
- 일부 플레이어에게는 그래픽이 낮게 느껴질 수 있음 / 엔드 콘텐츠가 지루할 수 있음 / 초보자에게는 진입장벽이 높을 수 있음

title: Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a story-driven action-adventure set in the aftermath of Order 66, following former Padawan Cal Kestis as he is forced out of hiding and drawn into a conflict with the Empire. It delivers a highly entertaining Star Wars experience built around strong atmosphere, memorable characters, satisfying progression, and an engaging narrative that captures the feeling of slowl

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a great action-adventure game where you play as a Jedi trying to survive the Empire. I really enjoyed the lightsaber combat, exploration, and learning new Force abilities. The story is emotional and Cal Kestis is a solid main character. Sometimes the platforming can feel a bit clunky, but overall it’s a very strong Star Wars experience. 8.5/10

8/10 I'm a huge fan of both Star Wars and Souls-like so I really love this game. The story of Cal Kestis is an interesting one as we get to see his development from disguising as on ordinary scrapper after Order 66, to fully embracing the force as a Jedi to rebuild the order. I've always been a fan of Star Wars and this game is a great bridge between Episode III and Episode IV! The graphics are beautiful, the story is compelling, and the characters are likeable. I got this game on sale a few months back and finally started it yesterday and now I'm hooked! 100% recommend giving this game a try. You won't be disappointed!

This game is really good. It feels kinda like a mixture of Elden Ring and Assassins Creed. My only real complaint with it is EA and their unwillingness to allow you to remap the controls on controller. I normally play with X as dodge, and RB as attack, but if you try to change it, the control scheme goes stupid and won't allow you to put different actions on the same button. It's just irritating.

if anyone hasn't played these yet you gotta do it. It's got that dark souls/life of p vibe where you time the blocks with the lightsaber. It's a great start to the trilogy. Can't wait for the 3rd one. Only gripe would be that this first one doesn't have fast travel so there are times where you just want to head back to the ship but have to back track. 8.5/10

스타워즈 팬이 아니었음에도 굉장히 재미있게 플레이했습니다 리뷰를 찾아봤을때 길찾기가 어렵고 복귀시 빠른이동이 없어 불편하다는 이야기가 있었는데 맵을 보면서 가면 길찾기 어렵지않고 복귀시 이동할때 안 가본길로도 가보고 전에는 못갔었던 곳을 이번에는 가면서 보상도 받고 하면서 굉장히 재미있게 했습니다 길찾기가 어렵다 하는 사람들은 아마 게임 1시간만 하고 내일하고 그런거같은데 쭉 이어서 하다보면 길이 자연스럽게 대충이라도 외워져서 스트레스 받지도 않습니다 단점이라고 할건 크게 없고 가끔씩 외형이나 모션이 이상하게 보이는게 있습니다만 그렇게 큰건 아닙니다 본인이 게임을 하고싶으면 사서 하시면 되고 1시간 깔딱하면서 도파민채우고 싶으면 다른거 하시면 됩니다 이상한 거짓음해에 속지마시고 믿고 하십쇼 19년도에 나온

i just loved this game ye some bits might be a lil basic like wall run here ect puzzles ect but ultimately it had me loving every bit of being a jedi and when it ended i did not even know it was coming i cant wait to play number 2. i got it on sale 100% worth it on sale ( get a controller)

It is a fantastic game with a great story, great characters and deep lore. The visuals are stunning and the lightsaber combat is very satisfying. However, the game is heavily dragged down by poor optimization. It suffers from frustrating random stuttering during gameplay and runs very hot. If you can look past the performance issues, the core experience is amazing.

대부분의 원작 영화를 기반으로 만든 게임들은 내용이 부실하거나 게임성이 구멍이 나는 경우가 많은데 이 게임은 진짜 잘 만든 게임임 스타워즈의 개성을 그대로 살리며 스토리도 좋고 게임성 또한 뛰어남 어릴 때 형들 어깨너머로 보던 트리플 a급 콘솔게임의 향기가 느껴졌음 최적화는 준수해서 30시리즈 정도면 충분히 렉 없이 플레이 가능 다만 난이도가 살짝 있어서 똥손인 분들은 참고하시고 80% 세일을 자주 해서 할인할 때 사는 걸 추천함

It's meh. Combat is repetetive and not engaging, and feels difficult more because it's clunky than actually hard. The worlds are super linear but want to give the illusion of an open world, which just makes the maps utterly confusing. EA should have just made a parcours game without the random slowdowns during what is normally extremely fast paced combat. They are trying to copy ubisofts assassins creed combat and that series should have ended after the first edition randomly brutal load screens

One enemy in particular, I tried to beat perhaps 6 or 7 times and came no where close, each time using up all 4 stimpaks (life recovery). Very reluctantly, to save my

Great story, fun worlds to explore but the combat system is VERY clunky and for a game that seems to rely on proper timing to avoid getting staggered to death and being unable to recover it doesn't lend well to it. The timing on the parry/dodge system seems all over the place, and 80% of the time if you miss a crucial dodge/parry you cannot recover and remained staggered until dead, and with some

A great IP cannot redeem an unpolished poorly designed game. Very unfortunate as the environment and musical score was beautiful.

Runs like gone off Bantha milk left in the twin suns for 3 weeks next to Uncle Ben. Tried many a "fix" to no avail, and not even running the game in extreme potato quality could remedy the severe lack of optimization this g

Unfortunately Fallen Order managed to string me along long enough in the beginning with cinematics and tutorials that by the time I realized this game was a total scam it was too late for me to refund it. So instead I finished it and out of spite am going to write a very long winded review that nobody will read. Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a game that does it's best to dress up lazy game makin

Absolutely horrible game, not worth more than $5. Every mission is a repeat of the previous one. Go to A, now run back to the ship. Combat is beyond janky and just boring, everything in the game is boring af. Combat full of animations and cheese, genuinely painful to play. Pathetic excuse for a dark souls copy.
세이브 포인트 간 이동 불가, 최악의 맵 디자인, 알아보기도 힘든 지도 시스템 수집품 찾을려면 맵 시작점 부터 끝까지 발로 걸어다녀야함

Love the star wars games, this games alright with the story and everything but the fighting just feels so clunky i played 1 plaaythrough tried to come back and play new game + and just reminded myself why i only played 1 playthrough

piece of crap of a game. no progression system. no feeling like your actually making your own jedi combat style and there is like 7 of them in the star wars universe. stupid mechanic with healing yourself, a bunch of soft locking bugs. and at the end it feels like you did the whole fing story for nothing. and the only hope i had while playing that the next game was better. i shall see but his is a

Absolutely the most frustrating, slowest, jankiest combat I have ever seen in a game. That on top of the fact that he is supposedly a Jedi? What were they thinking. Anyway, story was fine but this guy has no aura

Clunky combat, Stupidly difficult. It is basically a Star Wars themed souls-like but not as beautiful and engrossing as something like Black Myth Wukong. The first couple of hours tricks you into thinking that this is a game like Uncharted or Tomb Raider. But by the time you realize that it isn't, you've already broken a controller and can no longer get a refund. UPDATE: Whoever designed that ches

Looks great, plays awful. How this game gets overwhelming positive reviews is beyond me. Whole lotta Star Wars fans, for sure! The lightsaber combat, which should be amazing, is reduced to button mashing with some basic positioning. Near zero skill required. Constant back tracking is a total chore. The "squeeze through the crack in the wall" gimmick is overused on the first world never mind the re
EA app is a big minus the game is fine, if you like star wars get it when it is on sale.
Fallen Order wants you to feel like a fighty, telepathic space priest, and for about 20 hours or so, it does that. Stick with it, and you might also find a cool, yellow lightsaber.

Despite its polish and best ideas, I wish I'd just reinstalled both Force Unleashed games, and I encourage anybody eager for a fun, Force-filled, third-person Star Wars experience this year to do the same.

Fallen Order is a decent alternative to Dark Souls if you’re looking for a less hardcore experience. It leaves a pleasant aftertaste thanks to a well-directed ending, which makes you wonder if this game could have been so much better if it fully embraced its inner Uncharted instead of trying to wear two hats.

As underwhelmed as I was by Fallen Order, I hope that its inevitable success will encourage EA to do more with this franchise, since it makes a powerful case for the resonance of Star Wars — if this mediocre casserole of concepts yanked from superior titles didn’t have the license attached, I doubt anyone would be talking about it.
Technical issues marr an otherwise slick adventure. A must for Star Wars fans.

With an excellent droid companion and entertaining lightsaber combat, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order could have been a truly forceful addition to the franchise. However, navigation problems and unnatural level design takes it back down a darker path.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order certainly tries, but as Yoda pointed out, you either do or do not. For the most part, it does -- but without the grace or precision of a full-fledged Jedi Knight. The apprentice is not yet the master.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order could have been a good, short, but intense action game, lasting about ten hours, but instead, it swallows the player in unnecessary back and forths, artificial obstacles, puzzles and chests hunts, which burden the rhythm and fun. We come out all the more frustrated by a predictable story that goes nowhere, as well as by its combat system very close to Sekiro's, but whi

It's good enough that its host of technical problems feels like an affront to what the game could have been, and to the hard work and talent--and there's a considerable amount of talent here--of the people who made it.
스타워즈의 광활한 우주, 세계관을 좋아하고 광선검 좋아하면 필수인 게임 근데 스토리게임치곤 플레이 타임이 아쉽긴함 그래서 할인할 때 구매하면 후회는 없는 게임일듯 그리고 포스가 당신과 함께하길

A very good game often overshadowed by its sequel, Jedi: Survivor. I would recommend playing this game before survivor as while it's not required it gives you a better understanding of the story and mechanics.

I've finally gotten around to playing this game after years of owning it. I have really loved the experience. I've always been searching for a Jedi Academy/Outcast type game and this is the closest thing to that. Although I wish it was more aligned with the extended universe/legends, I think the story they made was fantastic. It plays into the movies, and the clone wars. The story is about 20 hour
Honestly a must have for any star wars fan, this game was BOMB! (tho it was kinda easy compared to other souls-likes in my opinion)
It is a mix of Uncharted + Lies of P. one of a single player game that should not be missed to play in this decade

if i could describe this game in one word. it would be incredible. taking elements from Sekiro and turning it into a Star Wars souls like was such a fun challenge with an amazing story. the combat is extremely fun also, its hard to get bored of throwing enemies into lava or off a cliff to their demise.

There are a few level designs that breaks immersion but still it's a decent game. I recall this had issues with optimization but modern PCs won't suffer this problem IMO. Overall it's a great game although it has some flaws.
exceptional game for star wars fans

[h1]새로운 제다이의 탄생[/h1] 기존 스타워즈 게임 시리즈의 핵앤슬래쉬에서 벗어나 [b]소울라이크 장르를 도입한 새로운 시리즈의 시작[/b], [b]Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order(스타워즈 제다이: 오더의 몰락)[/b]입니다. 플레이어는 66호 명령 이후 간신히 살아남은 젊은 파다완 [b]칼 케스티스[/b]가 되어, 5년간의 은둔 생활이 발각되며 제국의 추적을 피해 제다이 오더를 재건하기 위한 여정을 떠나게 됩니다. [hr][/hr] [h2]전투와 탐험[/h2] 전투는 소울라이크 스타일을 차용하여 체력 관리, 패링, 스태미너 기반의 공격 타이밍이 핵심입니다. 라이트세이버의 무게감과 타격감은 매우 뛰어나며, 싱글 블레이드 듀얼 블레이드 이도류 등 여러 공격 방식을 활용할 수 있고, 각

첫번째로 한 스타워즈 게임이자 두번째로 한 소울라이크 작품입니다. 조작감이 별로고 맵 보는게 불편했지만 다른 소울라이크 한번 하고 오니까 오더의 몰락은 친절한 게임이었네요. 스타워즈 컨셉을 잘 살렸고 BD-1이 귀엽고 리스폰의 연출덕에 구경거리가 많으니 재밌었습니다. 너무 추천~ 1회차 마무리 후 투기장 한바퀴 돌고 지웠습니다. 미니pc내장그래픽으로도 원활하게 잘 했습니다.

fun game! just got it on pc and wanted to play it again. The platforming feels a bit clunky but still competent. The Story is really solid, nothing revolutionary but a great start to a trilogy. highly above avrage game 7/10

I did have some random bugs where I would get stuck in an eternal loop, I also put it on easy for the inquisitor battles because after grinding for 3 hours on one fight, I got a little bored. All that said, this is a super addicting game, love the star wars atmosphere, feels like a worse version of modern KOTOR. pipeline_summary:
스타워즈 제다이: 몰락한 오더는 매력적인 스토리, 귀여운 BD-1 캐릭터, 그리고 재미있는 전투로 평가되며, 다만 길찾기와 조작감에 어려움을 겪을 수 있습니다.
장점: 매력적인 스토리와 그래픽으로 몰입감이 좋습니다. / 풍부한 콘텐츠와 귀여운 BD-1 캐릭터로 재미있습니다. / 좋은 퀄리티의 게임으로 가성비가 좋습니다.
단점: 길찾기에 어려움을 겪을 수 있습니다. / 조작감에 아쉬움이 있을 수 있습니다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 스타워즈 팬들을 위한 만족스러운 게임이지만, 길찾기와 퍼즐, 클렁키한 조작감 등이 단점으로 지적됨

장점: / 몰입감 있는 스타워즈 세계관과 스토리 / 만족스러운 라이트세이버 액션과 전투 / 우수한 그래픽과 사운드

단점: / 답답한 길찾기와 퍼즐 / 클렁키한 조작감과 플랫폼킹 / 불편한 맵 디자인과 미니맵 / 제한된 패딩과 세이브 포인트 이동 불가

title: God of War Ragnarök
sample_texts: 10/10 One of the best games I've ever played, and some of the most mature and nuanced storytelling in any medium.

정말 재미 있었습니다. 전작에 비해서 볼륨감이 확실히 커졌습니다. 옵션에서 퍼즐 난이도 조절도 가능해서 전작에 비해 퍼즐 관련 스트레스는 없었습니다. 앙그르보다 인지 뭔지 하는 소녀 관련 스토리 때, 갓 오브 워 게임 인생 처음으로 하차 할뻔, 했습니다. AAA급 게임 하다가 졸았던 것도 처음 입니다. 이 스토리가 30% 정도 진행 했을 때 나와서 진짜 고비 입니다. 올 스킵 권장 드립니다. 내용 1도 의미 없음.
oh my god this game actually made me cry it gave me so much emotion despite me barely being able to feel them which is weird because i have apathy but its so good!
Fun game, but memory leak means I choose to restart every ~30 mins to avoid the lag.
앙그로보다 파트 존나 지루함 진짜 이파트 빼고는 다 할만하고 재미있음

This games great, it adds onto the combat from the last game and its greatly optimized especially for low end computers like mine. I get a consistent 60-90 fps the whole time I'm playing and its never crashed on me. Def recommend playing God of War 2018 first before playing this one.
good overall but i kept getting bifrost all over me and nobody was there to talk about it. seems like a missed opportunity
theres nothing wrong about this game besidies some of the glitches in ironwood
If you have played GoW then this is just as good as any other GoW game. A beautiful continuation of an amazing story!

I thought it would hard to beat the first game but they did it and made it even better and even grew the son and father bond even more which i loved and if u love the lore of norse mythology this is the best game for you

What. a. game. Ragnarok is what many games aspire to be: it's terrific storytelling is something truly unprecedented, even in its own franchise. The characters and locations both feel alive and complete, creating true emotional attachment to them that lasts beyond the game's storyline. What Ragnarok does should be an example to future games and gamemakers alike, truly a 10/10 if I've ever seen one

God of War Ragnarök ⭐ Recommended 33.4 hours played Kratos and I were having a beautiful father-son journey across the Nine Realms. We slayed gods. We cried. We yelled “BOY” at things. Then Windrose came into my life and I haven’t opened this game in months. If you’re reading this Kratos, I’m sorry. The sea called. Atreus, tell your dad I love him. 12/48 achievements is just enough to look like I

[h1]8.5 / 10[/h1] 전작의 강점을 업그레이드했지만, 약점도 답습해버린 아쉬움. 중간중간 맥이 끊기는 부분이 있음에도 완성도를 해치는 정도라고 느껴지진 않습니다. [h1]훌륭한 수미상관[/h1] 유럽 신화의 시작과 끝을 관통하며, 크레토스의 신으로서의 고뇌와 아버지로서의 성장을 완벽에 가까운 수미상관으로 마무리 지었습니다. 전작에서 뿌려둔 복선들을 회수하며 '피로 얼룩진 과거'를 뒤로하고 새로운 길을 제시하는 서사는 묵직한 감동을 줍니다. 한 시대를 풍미한 주인공에게 이보다 더 깔끔하고 명예로운 예우가 있을까 싶을 정도로 서사가 정교합니다. [h1]웅장한 액션 시퀀스 연출[/h1] 게임 시작부터 입에서 '개쩐다'라는 소리가 절로 나옵니다. 압도적인 스케일의 연출과 화면을 가득 채우는 박진감은 소니
스포 없음)전작에서도 단점이라고 지적 받았던 몬스터 돌려막기는 이번작에서도 여전함.그럼에도 불구하고 두 주인공 캐릭터의 성장에 포커스를 둔 스토리라인은 근래 했던 게임중 가장 인상깊었음.

전작보다 많이 아쉬움. 몹들의 패턴이 더 악랄해져서 전작의 그 시원시원한 전투는 사라졌음. 스토리도 연출과 내용은 좋지만 개연성은 별로. 풀어가는 방식이 너무 가족 중심적으로 바뀜. GOW 스토리만의 장점을 죽이고 차기작만을 위해서 짜 맞추는 느낌. 판만 벌려 놓고 정작 개연성을 챙기지 못했음. pc판 오류인지 모르겠는데 특정 부분에서 버벅이거나 깜빡거리는 현상이 자주 있음. 잔버그가 좀 많음. 퍼즐 자체는 그렇게 안 어려운데 상호작용 가능 물체를 알아보기 어렵게 만듦. 틀린 그림 찾기 하는 기분임. 솔직히 평작보다 조금 나은 정도임.
갓오브워 북유럽 신화 시리즈의 아름다운 마무리 가족이라는 키워드로 전작과 함께 뛰어난 스토리 진행을 보여준다. 각각 매력있는 캐릭터들의 서사가 쌓일 수록 경험의 가치는 높아졌다. 크래토스의 여러 의미로써의 성장이 울림을 준다

전작 갓오브워에서 아쉬웠던 적들의 종류도 늘고 맵탐방과 수집물 수집 현황을 확인하기 쉬워진 느낌이여서 수집물 수집하는 과정에 담긴 북유럽 신화와 크레토스의 그리스 썰과 미미르의 썰을 듣는 소소한 재미가 더욱 커져서 좋았다. 무기도 다양하고 무엇보다도 맵 배경과 그에 어울리는 웅장한 음악도 훌륭한게 괜히 메타크리틱 90이 아니구나 라는 생각이 컸다. 유일하게 아쉬운점이라면은 9개의 영역중에서 아스가르드나 요툰헤임이 중점으로 스토리가 흐를 줄 알았는데 생각보다 좀 많이 탐방을 하지 못해서 아쉽다. 그래도 dlc 발할라를 무료로 얹어줘서 크레토스의 그리스 시절 무기와 순수 파밍이아닌 전투만 담백하게 만든 로그라이트인데 분량도 짧지 않아서 즐기기 좋았다. 시원한 액션과 적당히 매콤한 전투난이도를 원하면 적극 추천하

일단 스토리,연출 너무 좋습니다 아버지와 아들이라는 주제로 전작을 이어나가며 풀어나가는 맛이 있고, 전투 또한 너무 재밌습니다 근데 퍼즐이 너무 많긴 해요 그니까 재밌으먼 되는데 풀맛 나는 퍼즐이 아니고 ㅈ같게하는 퍼즐이 많습니다 그리스 사가때 퍼즐은 뭔가 화면의 구성을 센스적으로 찾는 퍼즐이면 이건 그냥 "하.. ㅅㅂ이거 굳이 왜 해야되지?" 이런 느낌이 많습니다 그리고 ㅅㅂ 보물찾기는 왜이렇게 많은 거죠? 오딘의 눈 찾다가 제 눈이 멀어서 제 눈을 찾아야 될 판입니다. 진짜 보물찾기는 감이 다 뒤져있습니다 하지만 이런 단점들 말고는 너무 재밌어요 결론은 추천합니다. 갓오브워 입문하시는 분은 이거 말고 전작부터 하셔야 스토리 이어집니다 참고하세용

God of War: Ragnarok on PC is yet another confirmation that expertly crafted ports are still possible, despite everything. Stunning graphics (though you’ll need a good PC to handle all that visual splendor), gameplay as sharp as Kratos’ blades, and a gripping narrative that won’t let you go. Sure, there was the risk of relying on past success and resting on its laurels. It doesn’t revolutionize, n

The cracks in its facade are stark because it’s otherwise such an incredibly vivid work, and the life breathed into it by its animators, artists, and actors is potent enough to survive some deeply odd writing and tonal choices. There’s a wonderful story, I’m sure, to be told about Kratos’s journey from destroyer to conciliator - the glimpses at mythological wonders that are allowed to exist in his

As far as the game itself is concerned, God of War Ragnarok could best be described as a safe sequel. Ragnarok focuses primarily on storytelling and big cinematic moments while refusing to take any chances when it comes to combat, exploration or other parts of the gameplay. Perfected craft distilled into a work of art.

Even if I don’t enjoy God of War Ragnarök’s cutscenes, having this second chance to play the game now that it’s out on PC has at least made me realize that the newer version of Kratos isn’t as much of a reboot as I previously thought. I wouldn’t say I love the new games as much as I did the originals, and I definitely would’ve enjoyed this game more if it felt a little more focused on action and l

God of War Ragnarok follows its predecessor’s path for its PC port, offering players the best version of the adventures of Kratos and Atreus. Of course the game takes advantage of the power of PCs and their exclusive technologies to push even further the visual quality, yet already impressive on PlayStation 5: native 4k, framerate unlocked, DLSS or FSR are particularly in the game to offer optimal

God of War Ragnarok on PC brings everything that made the PlayStation version fantastic, and visually, it even surpasses it. Even if you've already played the game, it's worth experiencing it again in its full glory.

If only all PC game ports were this polished. Ragnarök runs smoothly on PC, with no crashes, offering all the post-launch content from the original game and introducing a few small but welcome updates. Definitely a thumbs up.

The port is beautiful and it’s hard to complain about its technical aspects. Unfortunately, the game itself, while still good, is not as fascinating as its predecessors. One simply expects more from a series with such reputation, especially when it comes to the joy of smashing things up.
I can say without a doubt that Ragnarok takes advantage of the PC’s power better than any of the other games from the Playstation family.

Even though it doesn't bring significant graphical improvements over the PS5 version and takes up a colossal amount of space on your hard drive, God of War Ragnarok on PC is a good port that has great image quality and decent performance.

God of War Ragnarok for PC establishes itself as a more than worthy version of Santa Monica Studio's acclaimed title. With solid performance, a good range of options and a few minor improvements, Kratos can now tackle fatherhood in style.
God of War Ragnarok is just as stunning as it was on PS5, and those wanting to enjoy such an incredible game now have the chance on PC.

The base of the PC port is good, there is good performance, although there are more various minor problems in various areas. Basically, if you enjoyed the first game with Kratos and want more from this world, don't miss the second one.

God of War: Ragnarök is quite a piece for its high visual quality and exceptional performance. It is not necessary to use DLSS or FSR to enjoy the game. Although playing with a mouse and keyboard can be difficult but it is a successful PlayStation port. The graphics, combined with the immersion provided by the Dualsense, make PC players feel comfortable experiencing this acclaimed franchise.

God of War: Ragnarök's PC port is certainly packed with features, and while most players won’t encounter some of the issues outlined above, the button prompts problem can remain an annoyance for those who like the Classic control scheme. It’s also possible some of the bugs we ran into were just strange accidents, as we cannot replicate them again aside from Atreus’ green hair on Deck. With that in

God of War: Ragnarok lands on PC with a porting that is perfect, prudent to the right point and wise beyond measure. Thanks to scalability and optimization, practically anyone can enjoy the exploits of Kratos and Atreus. If you also have a recent gaming PC, then your journey into the myth will be absolutely unforgettable.

With all the video game movies being made these days (some good some bad)… it is not surprising that God Of War is joining their ranks. The best thing is whether the movie is a success or not… you can play God Of War Ragnarok and feel like you are playing a movie right now! If you’ve enjoyed the previous games or want to see what it’s all about… grab a copy. It’s a brilliant game on many levels an

God of War Ragnarök comes to PC with a very polished and well-executed version if we talk about performance level, performing fantastically well on a high-end PC and also doing a good job on a more modest computer such as one of the "Steam Deck" pocket computers that are now present on the market.

God of War Ragnarök for PC is the ultimate way to experience this epic Norse adventure without the need for a monster PC. In terms of graphics and performance, the game rarely drops a stitch, but the PC port manages to elevate the already sky-high level of the PlayStation version to an even higher standard.
God of War Ragnarök isn't the cleanest sequel, but it leaves Sony's angriest franchise in a great place.
A fabulous port of a fabulous game, God of War Ragnarok combines great story telling, great action, and great graphics to deliver a top notch experience.
pipeline_summary:
갓 오브 워 라그나로크는 뛰어난 그래픽스와 조작감, 몰입감 있는 전투와 스토리를 제공하는 게임입니다.
장점: 그래픽스와 조작감이 뛰어나다는 평가를 받았습니다. / 몰입감과 재미를 높이 평가했습니다. / 완성도와 수미상관을 높이 평가했습니다. / 탐험 콘텐츠를 제공한다는 평가를 받았습니다.
단점: 전작과 비슷한 느낌이라는 평가를 받았습니다. / 한 리뷰는 전투가 약간 지루해졌다고 평가했습니다. / 개연성이 부족하다는 평가를 받았습니다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 갓 오브 워 라그나로크는 크레토스와 아트레우스의 부자 관계와 북유럽 신화를 배경으로 한 엄청난 스토리텔링과 게임플레이를 제공하는 액션 어드벤처 게임이다.

장점: 
- 엄청난 스토리텔링과 캐릭터 개발
- 개선된 전투 시스템과 그래픽
- 방대한 오픈 월드와 다양한 사이드 퀘스트
- 감동적인 부자 관계와 캐릭터들

단점: 
- 일부 플레이어에게 지루할 수 있는 퍼즐 요소
- 그래픽과 성능 문제 (특히 PC 버전에서)
- 일부 미니게임과 보스전이 비판을 받음

[h1]돈에 환장한 IOI의 상술에 가려진 훌륭한 잠입 액션[/h1] HITMAN WoA는 전설적인 암살자 에이전트47이 되어 전 세계를 무대로 비밀 결사 프로비던스를 추적하고 타겟을 암살하는 과정을 다룹니다. 기존 3부작을 통합한 본작을 플레이한 후 지극히 주관적인 관점에서 장단점 리뷰를 작성해 보려 합니다. [b]장점[/b] [list] [*] 환골탈태한 최적화와 버그 수정: HITMAN 출시 당시엔 최적화 이슈를 비롯해 온갖 버그들이 난무했지만, 브랜드 통합 이후 HITMAN3까지 오면서 버그 수정은 물론 최적화도 훌륭하게 잘 잡았다. [*] 초심자를 위한 배려: 다소 복잡할 수 있는 잠입과 암살 시스템을 튜토리얼을 통해 초심자들도 쉽게 적응할 수 있도록 보다 잘 다듬었다. [*] 압도적인 볼륨과 리플

Hitman’s Freelancer mode is something rare: an intoxicating blend of challenge and approachability. It plays on the hubris of longtime players, but also guides newcomers with thematic objectives and a more explicit overall structure. It may not allow for the micro-repetition that makes the base trilogy tick. But it does maintain a rapid momentum from the beginning of each run to its bitter, comica

While Freelancer Mode introduces a lot of new elements to the table, it also brings a myriad of bugs and issues that can take away from the overall experience. The freelancer mode is by no means bad, but it definitely has issues executing its concept due to its flawed RNG and mechanics.
The story is solid, but the gameplay is where it truly shines. The freedom to tackle missions however you want makes it one of the most unique games on the market.

Hitman: World of Assassination brings together most of the trilogy’s content in a convenient package. While what’s excluded and the always online requirements are baffling, the game itself is still a ton of fun with the new Freelancer mode bringing you to locations from all three games. This is a great way to dive in for newcomers, though veterans will be confused as to what’s not included and why

With any roguelike game, the biggest problem is repetition. Hitman: Freelancer is in a strange place when it comes to this; the core gameplay loop and these levels have, in some cases, been around since 2016, and repetition is built into the game's framework. Hitman World of Assassination은 잠입 암살 게임으로, 공식 한글 패치가 없어 아쉽다는 의견이 있지만, 재미와 그래픽을 높이 평가받고 있습니다.
장점: 다양한 루트가 존재해 루트대로 깨는 것이 더 재미있다는 의견도 있습니다. / 핵심 게임플레이가 매우 만족스럽습니다. / 최적화와 현실감이 좋다는 반응이 있습니다. / 플레이 흐름을 익힌 뒤 클리어까지 이어졌다는 반응이 있습니다.
단점: 구매 과정이 복잡하고 정리가 안 돼 있어 구매 가이드가 필요하다는 의견도 있습니다. / 한 리뷰는 게임의 진행 장벽이 어려울 수 있으며, CQB가 되어버리는 경우도 있습니다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 히트맨 월드 오브 어싸시네이션은 풍부한 콘텐츠와 높은 자유도로 호평받는 잠입 액션 게임입니다.

장점: 
- 높은 자유도와 창의성을 제공하는 잠입 액션 게임플레이
- 다양한 암살 방법과 도구, 무기, 복장 등 풍부한 콘텐츠
- 세련된 그래픽과 사운드 디자인
- 매력적인 스토리와 캐릭터

단점: 
- 복잡하고 불친절한 구매 구조와 DLC 정책
- 온라인 연결 요구 사항으로 인한 불편함
- 일부 미션과 콘텐츠가 제한되어 있음

title: Dragon Age: Inquisition
sample_texts: I love this game, it has plently of flaws (Having mutiple open worlds in a slow paced tac rpg was an insane choice) but man the music, visuals, writing, characters etc are just so wonderful and iconic i can't help but love it, an absoloute classic and i can't recomend it enough even if you haven't played the originals.

I don't understand why people don't vibe with this game. It's very fun, super entertaining, feels like it combines 3 of my favorite games, Oblivion Remastered, Baldur's Gate 3, and World of Warcraft, truly a phenomenal game, give it a try!

모드 매니져에 한글패치 적용해서 모드로 플레이 실행해서 하는중. 게임 패드 인식이 안되서 키마로 하다가, 게임 설정에서 게임 패드로 바꿔줘야 되더라. 게임패드로 하는게 더 재밌음. 1.첫 번째 보스 잡을 때 그냥 체력 돼지라 좋같았음 2.다른 캐릭터들도 조종할 수 있는데, 인물들이랑 대화할 때 주인공 목소리가 나와서 몰입 안되서 걍 주인공만 쓰게 됌. 전술 화면에 들어가서 애들 명령내리는것도 걍 하기 귀찮음, 게임 소개에서도 하기 귀찮으면 걍 하나만 조종하라니까 대충 캐릭터 전술 설정만 함. 3.와우같은 붕쯔붕쯔한 액션이지만 나름 레트로한 매력이랑 여럿이서 다니니까 안외롭고 분위기 괜찮음. 4.오픈월드는 꽤 잘만들었음 B급 반지의제왕 느낌 5.초반 테이젠의 기록물인가 뭔가 찾으려면 나침반 울릴 때 몇 번 눌

긴 대장정이 끝나고 마침내 엔딩을 봤습니다. 게임을 약 2시간 정도 플레이한 후 저는 이 게임이 어떻게 GOTY를 받았는지 의문을 갖지 않을 수 없었습니다. 그만큼 불편한 점이 산더미처럼 쌓여있었으며 혁신적인 요소도 찾아볼 수 없었습니다. 보통 GOTY를 받았다면 불편함을 감수하고도 게임을 즐겨야만 하는 강점을 가지고 있었으나 이 게임은 그렇지 않습니다. 오히려 이도저도 아닌 어중간한 포지션에 끼어 있는 게임입니다. 게임의 엔딩을 보고 난 후 여전히 GOTY급 게임인지에 대해선 의문이 가득하지만 게임에 내세우는 재미 정도는 확실하게 있다는 생각으로 바뀌었습니다. 세미 오픈월드는 짧고 멍청한 서브퀘스트와 잡일, 반복 임무로 채워져 있고 조작감은 구리며 어색한 모션에 전투는 미묘합니다. 그럼에도 스토리는 꽤 재

This game is a classic. Must play. 10/10

2014년 고티 드래곤에이지 인퀴지션 사고나서 이제서야 해봤는데 명작은 시대를 가리지 않는다는 말이 딱 느껴지더라 2011년 고티 스카이림이 지금해도 재미있고 2015년 고티인 위쳐3가 아직도 언급되듯이 아무리 빈집털이 고티라 불리는 2014년 고티라도 충분히 고티이름값은 하는겜이라 생각된다 혹시라도 인퀴지션 해볼생각있으면 한패랑 아이템줏을때 모션 지우는 모드랑 워테이블 시간 줄이는모드 달리기 모드는 깔고해라 저 모드들 있고 없고 차이가 너무 커서 바닐라로 하라고 하기에는 암걸릴거라
This is a game that challenges your moral compass and help teach you that there are more paths out there than you know. Overall, the game has a challenging and incredible story. 5 stars!

Dragon Age: Inquisition feels like the kind of RPG that slowly takes over your routine without you even noticing. What begins as curiosity quickly turns into dozens of hours spent exploring valleys, climbing ruins, listening to party banter and making decisions that genuinely feel like they carry weight. Still After all this time i think Inquisition was a Great end for the Series.
Probably the best after Origins. So well written. Fantastic characters. Sera, Cole, and Dorian are all just so cute.

My No.1 Dragon Age game. The companions are fun and interesting, and the story feels larger and more impactful than previous games. And honestly, Solas as a character was a very nice touch.
one of my comfort games another one is TESV

[h1] 버그가 망쳐버린 대작 [/h1] 이라는 한 마디로 표현하고 싶습니다. 제가 왠만해서는 버그 가지고 심각할 정도로 뭐라고 하진 않습니다. 보통 한 번 정도 일어날 해프닝이기도 하고, 게임 전반으로 영향을 주는 버그가 아닌 이상 그렇게 자주 터지는 편도 아니니까요. 근데 얘는 선을 좀 많이 넘었습니다. 게임 진행하면서 치명적인 버그가 2개씩이나 있었고, 결국 침입자 DLC 하다가 하차했습니다. 제 억울함을 한 번 들어주시죠. 하나는 게임을 할 때마다 로딩 후 짧으면 5분, 길면 2시간 단위로 게임과 그래픽카드가 충돌이 나며 게임이 멈춥니다. 아니 사실 말이 글카와 충돌이 난다는 거지 아직까지도 원인이 뭔지를 모르겠습니다. 글카나 드라이버도 꿀릴 정도의 사양이나 버전은 아니고,(9070XT) 멈출 때마다

Dragon Age: Inquisition is a weird amalgamation of two different games. One is almost like those classic RPGs from BioWare’s early years; it makes you sit on the edge of your chair, waiting for shocking twists, deliberate over moral dilemmas, laugh and cry. The other is a MMO with generic quests and soulless grinding, having slithered where it should not be. And, alas, there is no way to separate
Rubbish controls, dull combat, and a general sense that it's not quite sure what it wants to be, let down an otherwise entertaining and regularly amusing world-saving romp.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is a frustrating game. I often found myself scratching and clawing through boring, MMO-like content for enough power points to progress to the next major plot event. However, the fact that I was willing to slog through it at all shows just how good this game can be when it hits its stride.

I found it to be a jack-of-all-trades, but a master of none. Because of this, you’re left having your attention continually and equally divided, forcing you to pour in tons of hours just to start feeling invested in the world.

More playground than living breathing world. BioWare opens a gigantic fantasy theme park. But unfortunately the only thing that is epic is the amount of content. Story, character behavior, quests and minigames are a mere shadow of the studio's former glory.

2026 - Not recommended I played Dragon Age: Origins. I liked it, I understood it, it was easy to learn (for me). I played it with different characters. I have a different story to tell about Inquisition: it is no longer the same system, I find it extremely cumbersome to control my team, I have no idea how to see all the items in my inventory. "Inventory full" - what?! How? How many items can I hav
고티고 나발이고 너무 지루하고 타격감도 없고 하..만원에 산것도 아깝네
Im not going to play a game i bought just to play a min clash of clans. Tell me why i have to wait for structures to be built. Terrible game and pathetic addition to any fantasy game

I'm sure this is a perfectly fine game, but the deal breakers for me were: Team management felt very chaotic and not streamlined enough for me. Camera controls don't have enough customization and it makes just getting from one place to another (and aiming) feel tedious and wonky. And the voice acting feels.inauthentic and disingenuous. In my opinion, there's no real aid the tactical camera angle g
Boring. I quit after 10 hours. Too much talking and wandering. Even on casual the combat is too difficult and no fun.
game so trash it made me go do my homework
Best Dragon Age so far, hands down. Pity about the naff writing. [Issue#257, p.51]
The masters over at Bioware have done it again. This epic journey into the world of Thedas will steal more than 100 hours of your time, hours so full of superb gaming that you'll never forget them.
Despite numerous shortcomings, the new BioWare project is undoubtedly worth your attention. One of the best RPGs of 2014.
RPG of the year and a must-have for everyone who values good story delivered in a cinematic fashion and supported by outstanding sound and graphics. [13/2014, p.58]
The most ambitious game BioWare has ever made. [Jan 2014, p.104]
A game to spend the whole winter with. The breathtaking and complex story is set in a huge open world, and despite its partial mistakes the game works pretty well. [Issue#248]

As 2014 draws to a close, Inquisition is a contender not only for game of the year, but for the best title to come out of BioWare’s impressive stable - either way, it’s an essential purchase for any RPG fan.
At the very least BioWare managed to present a workable concept of an RPG which is completely detached from the legacy of pen-and-paper RPG. [Jan 2015, p.52]
The best of the Dragon Age series by far and one of BioWare's most accomplished games to boot. [Jan 2015, p.50]

Huge amount of content, many possible choices with distinct consequences, reappearance of beloved characters. Despite some obvious flaws and various shortcomings, Dragon Age: Inquisition certainly feels like a step in the right direction for the series.
Inquisition is an excellent return to form after the claustrophobic, rushed Dragon Age 2, an exciting and sprawling colossus of a game that rewards the player at every turn.

Excellent RPG beats both of its predecessors and offers the unspoiled entertainment for hundreds of hours. The sophisticated world of Dragon Age finally comes alive in all aspects and incredible character and story detail fully compensates a bit overused plot about saving the world.
Inquisition is a good game that suffers from a lack of editorial judgment and polish.
Despite some minor hiccups, Inquisition is absolutely the realest of deals, and should be missed at your own peril.

Dragon Age Inquisition is not a common RPG: it's the furious scream of Bioware, strong enough to make windows and walls rattle. It is a demonstration of skill and power that proves that the Canadian developer still knows how to put heart and brain into their projects.

For people looking to get lost in a detailed world brimming with political intrigue, warring factions, tough choices that affect the story and gobs of replayability that will last you well beyond Fall and into the new year, Dragon Age Inquisition is a must buy.

Dragon Age: Inquisition makes up for the way too short Dragon Age 2. The gameworld is huge and tremendously diverse. The franchise seems to go a bit less dark and intense, but it introduces a new blockbuster RPG standard that many developers will struggle to meet.
It’s a welcome return to form for a franchise that felt adrift after Dragon Age 2, and is easily recommendable to RPG fans who have a spare few weeks to dedicate to a single game.
From a content point of view is simply astonishing but the game has several problems with the combat system starting from the tactical pause (and view) to the action aspect of the fights.
It's the only RPG you'll need this winter and finally a worthy heir to Origins, last generation's best role-playing game.
BioWare has created a role-playing game which feels like a massive monument to our culture. Inquisition is an absolute blast to experience for one hour or 150.
Graphics, gameplay, scale, storytelling, depth, content and the finish: Dragon Age: Inquisition proves that it can bring each of these components together without making any concessions.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is the best installment in the series and one of the best games in BioWare's history. There's a lot to praise: an interesting story, cool characters, big world, Skyhold. Sure, there are some minor issues and the combat system isn't perfect but the scale and attention to detail make all of that seem unimportant. An excellent game indeed.

Inquisition offers tons of rpg-gameplay-elements. The classic high-fantasy-setting combines action- and tactical combat, rich characters with lots of dialogue-options. On top you’ll find yourself as a leader of the inquisition-faction who has to build up a majestic stronghold. Graphics and sound are great, but technical issues like glitches and bugs with animations are also part of the game. Menus
Inquisition gets under your skull like red lyrium.

It’s a great game from start to finish, one that lets you do just about whatever you want, and then rewards you for just about all of it. While it does have some minor problems, they don’t detract from the quality of the whole. The way the world responds to, and builds itself around, your character makes Inquisition a must-play for any RPG enthusiast.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is still a brilliant if flawed gem from one of the best storytellers in the genre. The journey is long. The path is fraught with dangers and more than a few missteps, but it’s an epic adventure well worth the undertaking.

It’s one of the most overwhelming experiences on the market, containing not only the best character development found in any RPG, but an open world that’s actually polished. The artistic and visual fidelity help with the immersion, but it’s the branching, player driven storyline and exquisitely layered combat system that create something special.

Can only be described as Bioware’s return to glory and as the preeminent force in the RPG genre. Dragon Age Inquisition is a must play for anyone who calls themselves a fan of role playing games. Dragon Age Inquisition, when or if it is finally completed will leave players with the overall sense that this game will replace a favorite movie and a beloved book as a source of contemplation for a long

Not only one of the most expansive RPGs I’ve ever played, but one of the few that successfully fills its gorgeous, massive world with meaningful things to do and see. A frustratingly vague plot and typical BioWare bugginess drag it down a bit, but both in combat and out, Inquisition marks a welcome return to the RPG depth that made Dragon Age: Origins and Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic so

How much you enjoy Inquisition will likely depend on what it is you enjoy about RPGs. If you want complex systems and hardcore challenge, it could potentially disappoint. I don’t. I want a rich world, interesting characters, and a dramatic and memorable plot. Judged on those criteria, Dragon Age: Inquisition sits happily alongside BioWare’s best.

Its not-so-fatal flaw is that in offering so much, both in terms of player choice and in going for peak-BioWare in every aspect of the game, those individual moments, characters, activities and plot beats often don't benefit from the focus and importance needed to unlock their full potential. Still, that's hardly a crime, and one more than made up for by the many high points

The biggest criticism that can be leveled at the title is that it is a little too conservative in its approach to the role-playing and action genres and fails to introduce something entirely new.At the same time, the developers have managed to carefully polish the core tenets of Dragon Age and fans of the series will be happy to discover how easy it is to tailor Inquisition to their own play style

An epic experience that haunts you between play sessions, enticing you to come back for more. It may not hit the lofty heights of Skyrim, but it comes bloody close and, unlike its most obvious comparison, boasts consistently fantastic characters, a memorable and compelling storyline, and breathtaking visual fidelity out of the box.

The brilliant part of this concept is how it imbues every activity with purpose. The world of Inquisition is immense, and a freeform structure means everything you accomplish, no matter how small, feeds your larger aspirations.

BioWare studio wanted to redeem itself for the not-so-convincing Dragon Age II. This episode is one of its finest productions. Amazing environments that take hours to get explored, greats quests with some hard decisions to make, fantastic characters and a great combat system: Dragon Age :Inquisition will probably become a masterpiece for anyone who's been in love with Dungeons & Dragons or Tolkien
Yes, Inquisition is an all around excellent game.A beautiful fantastic world, a great roleplaying game - but not as good as Dragon Age: Origins
The first Dragon Age still is the best game of the franchise, but we've really enjoyed this one. Fans of the genre won't be disappointed.

Dragon Age: Inquisition is BioWare's new masterpiece and they weren't bluffing. The RPG has everything a fan of the genre could wish for: an amazing amount of gameplay, a great story and extreme beauty. A must-have for everyone that calls himself a gamer.

Bioware is back with sheer passion and love for its work. It demonstrates so offering a new way for players to enjoy strategic RPGs, with a battle system that merges action and tactics, and it plays smoothly whether on PC or consoles. Add to this an incredible amount of gameplay, free roaming and an interesting intrigue, and what you get is one of the best games of this year.
pipeline_summary:
강한 만족감을 주는 게임으로 기억된다는 반응이 있습니다.
장점: 다양한 캐릭터와 방대한 콘텐츠로 몰입감이 높은 게임이다. / 패드 지원을 통해 접근성이 높아졌다. / 강한 만족감을 주는 게임으로 기억된다는 반응이 있습니다. / 오래 기억할 만큼 강한 만족감을 줬다는 반응이 있습니다.
단점: 버그로 인해 게임 진행이 끊길 수 있다. / 반복적인 플레이로 인해 지루할 수 있다.
baseline_summary:
한줄평: 드래곤 에이지 인퀴지션은 방대한 오픈월드와 다양한 캐릭터, 깊이 있는 스토리를 갖춘 우수한 RPG이지만, 일부 플레이어에게는 버그와 부자연스러운 조작감으로 인해 불만족스러운 경험을 제공할 수 있습니다.

장점: 
- 방대한 오픈월드와 자유도 높은 탐험 
- 깊이 있는 스토리와 캐릭터 
- 다양한 동료 캐릭터와 상호작용 
- 전략적 전투 시스템 
- 아름다운 그래픽과 사운드

단점: 
- 일부 플레이어에게 나타나는 다양한 버그 문제 
- 부자연스러운 캐릭터 조작감 
- 복잡한 메뉴와 인터페이스 
- 일부 미션의 지루함과 반복적인 요소

title: Battlefield 2042

Battlefield 2042 arrived with enormous expectations. Developed by DICE and published by Electronic Arts, the game promised massive warfare, dynamic weather events, and the next evolution of the long-running Battlefield formula. What players received at launch, however, was one of the franchise’s most divisive entries. At its best, Battlefield 2042 captures the cinematic chaos the series is famous

After playing Battlefield 2042, I can say the game delivers some really intense and cinematic multiplayer moments. The large-scale battles, explosions, vehicles, and weather effects make matches feel chaotic and exciting, especially when everything is happening at once. The graphics and sound design are impressive, and the modern setting gives access to fun weapons, gadgets, and vehicles. Some map

Despite the awful launch, EA has, for some reason, decided to stick with this game. In my eyes, it has redeemed itself with the addition of Team Deathmatch (my favorite game mode), the removal of most bugs (I haven't encountered any since I started playing), the addition of new guns, cool cosmetics like Isaac from Dead Space, and the promise of continued support. However, it lacks a campaign, whic

Battlefield 2042 is actually pretty fun now and the online is still active, so finding matches isn’t a problem. The gameplay can get really chaotic in a good way and some moments feel insane with all the explosions and vehicles everywhere. The only thing is the game feels really grindy sometimes like it wants you to play 24/7, and there are still a few bugs here and there. Still a solid Battlefiel
It had a rocky launch but its got a lot better and very worth it for how cheap it can be on sales

Battlefield 2042는 빡세게 경쟁하기보다는, 편하게 즐기기 좋은 전장 느낌의 FPS였다. 요즘 FPS들은 한 판만 해도 진이 빠질 정도로 경쟁 중심인 경우가 많은데, Battlefield 2042는 아님. 물론 잘하면 좋지만, 이 게임은 순수하게 “전쟁 난장판” 자체를 즐기는 맛이 크다. 탱크 지나가고, 헬기 날아다니고, 건물 무너지고, 사방에서 총알 날아오는 상황 속에서 정신없이 뛰어다니다 보면 이 게임만의 분위기가 있다. 특히 대규모 전투 특유의 스케일이 정말 인상적이었다. 혼자 엄청 캐리해야 한다는 부담보다는, 팀 전체 전장 속 한 명이 되어 자유롭게 플레이하는 느낌에 가깝다. 그래서 가끔은 진지하게 하기보다 친구들이랑 웃으면서 하기 좋다. 갑자기 차량에 치이고, 낙하하다 폭발에 휘말리고,
It's a mid game but for 2 bucks it is basically free
great game different then most Battlefield games but overall its a great game

Game is pretty playable for current state and I enjoyed it, I think overall there's so many map are really good for air combat which is what I love, but controlling jets are kinda more difficult than other bf games. I think it's a great game on 90 or 95% discount but not on the normal price.

LOVE THIS GAME i didnt want to spend 70+ dollars on COD so when it went on sale i got it and it was better then i thought some of my settings got messed up but it wasnt that hard to fix you can run the game easly i have a 2060 super and i5-9400F 16GB DDR4 ram and i can run the game easly at 120 fps at high settings some times i can get 144 but when i am playing and there a lot of stuff happaing i
love this game. good as long as you have a good pc.

Need to be transparent - the one dollar sale is doing a lot of work in this review. Look, 2042 launched in a state that genuinely warranted the reception it got. DICE shipped something that was missing features the series had carried for years, the specialists system felt like a fundamental misread of what Battlefield players actually wanted, and the maps were so large they occasionally felt empty
Enjoyable even in wireless remote locations

Great FPS game that's more like a crossover of Call of Duty and Battlefield rather than traditional BF gameplay. I love it, great game filled with people complaining that they can't shoot straight and that it's "unrealistic". It is a video game; that is very very fun.

출시 초기 유저들의 큰 분노를 샀던 각종 문제들은 배틀필드 프랜차이즈의 종말을 가져오는 듯했으나, 지금은 완벽하지는 않아도 상당 부분 훌륭하게 봉합되었습니다. 할인 기간에 구매한다면 가격 부담도 매우 적으며, 2042의 사후 지원이 공식적으로 끝난 만큼 더 이상의 치명적인 버그 발생 없이 시스템적으로 안정적인 플레이가 보장됩니다. 다만 배틀필드 6의 출시로 인해, 현재 2042의 게임 내 활력은 많이 떨어진 상태입니다. 피크 타임에 잘 나올 때도 겨우 5,000명을 넘기고, 사람이 없을 때는 1,000명대에 머무르고 있습니다. 이러한 매치메이킹의 한계를 감안하고 세일할 때 구매한다면, 돈값은 충분히 하고도 남습니다. 좋은 의미로든 나쁜 의미로든, 이토록 거대한 전장의 스케일은 오직 2042에서만 경험할 수
One of my favourites of this franchise. Definitely worth grabbing if on sale.
One of the weakest BF game but all in all 128 player can still be fun
